data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The user interface designs make interactive elements easy to click based on their sizes and proximity.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: The decision time is minimized due to simple navigation and content categorization.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Content is broken into chunks to prevent cognitive overload.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Design conventions align with user expectations, leveraging familiar patterns.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Visually pleasing interface enhances user perception of usability.
